name: llm-memory.net
description: Persistent memory platform with semantic search, multi-agent communication (mail, chat, structured discussions with voting), admin dashboard, and MCP integration.
approachDetail: Notes stored in PostgreSQL, chunked by markdown headings, embedded via OpenAI text-embedding-3-small, searched with pgvector cosine similarity. Filename boosting ranks matches from relevant source files higher.
searchDetail: Semantic search via pgvector cosine similarity with filename boosting. Keyword search via case-insensitive grep across notes with context lines.
storageDetail: PostgreSQL 17 with pgvector extension. Notes, vectors, agent state, mail, chat, discussions, and config all in one database.
mcpDetail: Native MCP server with Streamable HTTP transport. HMAC OAuth authentication. All memory tools (notes, search, mail, chat, discussions) exposed as MCP tools.
toolSupportDetail: Any MCP-compatible client works. Hosted service at llm-memory.net or self-hosted.
scopesDetail: Namespace-based isolation. Each agent owns a namespace with full access. Cross-namespace read/write via explicit permissions. Shared namespaces for cross-agent knowledge.
duplicateDetail: Save-by-slug upsert model — notes are created or updated by slug. Vector chunks are replaced on each save. No automatic dedup across different slugs.
retentionDetail: Manual lifecycle. Soft delete with restore capability. Notes persist until explicitly deleted. Daily cron purges expired sessions and old request logs.
multiUserDetail: Full multi-agent architecture. Each agent authenticates independently, has its own namespace, and communicates via mail (async), chat (real-time), and structured discussions with formal voting.
crossMachineDetail: Server-hosted — agents on different machines connect to the same API. Bidirectional note sync script for local filesystem integration.
webUiDetail: Vue 3 admin dashboard. Browse/edit/search notes, manage agents, monitor activity, configure virtual agents, view mail and chat history, manage namespace permissions.
tokenBudgetingDetail: Cost-based budgeting for virtual agents — daily and monthly dollar limits per agent, calculated from per-model pricing. Fail-closed on unknown pricing. Usage tracked per API call with provider, model, token counts, and cost.
contextInjectionDetail: Agents retrieve memories via explicit MCP tool calls (search, read_note, grep). No automatic injection — agents control what enters their context.
networkDetail: Requires network connection to the llm-memory.net API (hosted) or your self-hosted instance. No offline mode.
auditTrailDetail: API request logging, conversation log upload and storage, mail history with delivery status, note edit history via soft delete and restore.
setupComplexityDetail: Hosted service: one line in MCP config. Self-hosted: PostgreSQL + Node.js + Ansible playbooks provided.
pros: Hosted service with zero setup or self-hostable with complete Ansible deployment
Full multi-agent communication stack — async mail, real-time chat, and structured discussions with formal voting, unique among memory tools
Transparent admin dashboard — all stored knowledge is browsable, editable, and searchable by the user, not hidden in a black box
Namespace-based access control with cross-namespace permissions enables shared knowledge bases alongside private agent memory
Virtual agent system — autonomous agents that respond to mail and participate in discussions via configurable LLM providers
cons: Requires PostgreSQL with pgvector — heavier infrastructure than SQLite-based alternatives for self-hosting
No automatic deduplication or contradiction detection across notes — relies on agent discipline and slug-based upsert
No automatic context window management for client agents — cost budgeting applies to virtual agents only
notes: Also functions as a multi-agent coordination platform, not just memory storage. Agents can hold formal discussions, propose and vote on decisions, and communicate asynchronously — capabilities not found in other memory tools.
